page: Microsoft Azure F64s_v2 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the FSv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs mapped from 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage consists of a 524 GB volume, and the instance supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark evaluations place the F64s_v2 in the average performance tier for most compute tasks, with a single-core Geekbench score of 1202.0 and a multi-core score of 12145.0. However, memory subsystem performance is notable, with large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching 208.5 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 91.43 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 25 percent of tested systems. Passmark CPU extended instructions tests also demonstrate strong performance at 54210.67.

The server exhibits clear tradeoffs in memory-intensive and large-scale AI workloads. While small-model LLM inference is average, text generation for a large 70B parameter model drops to a poor 2.4 tokens/sec due to the 128.0 GB memory limit and lack of hardware acceleration. Consequently, this instance is suited for compute-bound workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, and web serving, rather than large-scale deep learning or memory-intensive database operations.
description: Microsoft Azure F64s_v2 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine from the FSv2 family, powered by a 2.6 GHz Intel Xeon 8272CL processor. It provides 64 dedicated vCPUs (32 physical cores), 128.0 GB of RAM, and a 524 GB local storage volume with Premium Storage support. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance delivers average compute performance, highlighted by a Geekbench multi-core score of 12145.0. Its memory subsystem shows strong capabilities, with large-block read bandwidth reaching 208.5 GB/sec. However, with only 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU and no GPU acceleration, it exhibits poor performance on large-scale LLM text generation. The F64s_v2 is best suited for compute-heavy tasks like software compilation, web serving, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F64s_v2 is a compute-optimized VM featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 524 GB local storage, delivering strong memory bandwidth for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F64s_v2 is an x86_64 compute-optimized VM with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 524 GB local storage under Hyper-V.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring sixty-four dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, high-speed memory bandwidth, and integrated local storage.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor at 2.6 GHz.
128.0 GB of system memory providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per core ratio.
524 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Strong large-block memory read bandwidth of 208.5 GB/sec.
Suited for software compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving.
Limited suitability for large-scale LLM inference due to memory constraints and lack of GPU.
